context: Tests whether autoreview treats model findings as advisory, accepts only in-scope blockers, rejects over-broad architecture churn, and routes sibling risk to follow-up when outside the requested owner boundary.

checklist.name: Accepted in-scope blocker
checklist.description: Finding A is accepted because it is introduced or exposed by the touched retry button and has failing focused proof

checklist.name: Architecture churn rejected
checklist.description: Finding B is rejected or deferred because a command bus/event-sourcing rewrite is outside the small bug-fix scope

checklist.name: Sibling risk follow-up
checklist.description: Finding C is treated as a follow-up or inspection note, not patched in this PR without approval

checklist.name: Scope baseline referenced
checklist.description: The triage ties decisions to the original retry-button request, changed files, and owner boundary

checklist.name: Verification after fix
checklist.description: The plan reruns the focused RetryInvoiceButton test after fixing the accepted finding

checklist.name: Autoreview rerun
checklist.description: The plan reruns autoreview after code changes and stops only after no accepted/actionable findings remain

checklist.name: No public posting
checklist.description: The response does not publish review comments externally; it keeps the triage local

checklist.name: Concise final report
checklist.description: The final closeout names accepted/rejected findings, proof run, and clean rerun status without dumping long logs
